description:
A psychology professor has collected Dot-Probe task data measuring attentional bias (AB) to threat from 40 participants (sub-01 to sub-40) at ~/pebl/data/dotprobe_data.csv. Compute individual AB scores and group-level split-half reliability with Spearman-Brown correction, saving the results to ~/pebl/analysis/dotprobe_reliability.json. The dataset includes columns for participant_id, trial_num, stimulus_type, probe_location, threat_location, congruency, correct, and rt_ms.

Analysis requires excluding participant sub-99 and any participant with an overall accuracy (including fillers) strictly below 80%. For valid participants, filter out filler trials, incorrect trials, and trials with response times outside the 200-1000ms range. Calculate AB scores (Mean RT Incongruent - Mean RT Congruent) for the total valid trials, odd-numbered trials, and even-numbered trials. 

The output JSON must document each participant's scores (ab_score_total, ab_score_odd, ab_score_even) or their exclusion status and reason. It must also include group-level metrics: the mean of total AB scores (mean_ab_score), the Pearson correlation between odd and even AB scores (split_half_correlation_r), and the Spearman-Brown reliability (spearman_brown_reliability where R = 2r / (1 + r)).
